openmeter/billing/charges/creditpurchase.purpose: Domain package for credit-purchase charge lifecycle: defines the Service interface (Create, GetByIDs, List, ListFundedCreditActivities, ExternalPaymentLifecycle, InvoicePaymentLifecycle), domain types (Charge, Intent, Settlement, Status), and the Handler interface that mediates all ledger side-effects. Settlement type (promotional/external/invoice) is a tagged-union with MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON.

patterns.name: Settlement tagged union
patterns.description: Settlement is a private-field struct with a SettlementType discriminator; construct via NewSettlement[T] and access via As*Settlement(). Never set t/invoice/external/promotional fields directly.
patterns.example: s := creditpurchase.NewSettlement(creditpurchase.ExternalSettlement{GenericSettlement: ..., InitialStatus: CreatedInitialPaymentSettlementStatus})

patterns.name: Intent.Normalized() before Validate()
patterns.description: Always call intent.Normalized() (which calls meta.Intent.Normalized() and NormalizeOptionalTimestamp) before Validate() to truncate timestamps to streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration and round CreditAmount to currency precision.
patterns.example: input.Intent = input.Intent.Normalized(); if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { ... }

patterns.name: Handler abstraction for ledger
patterns.description: All ledger writes must go through the creditpurchase.Handler interface methods (OnPromotionalCreditPurchase, OnCreditPurchaseInitiated, OnCreditPurchasePaymentAuthorized, OnCreditPurchasePaymentSettled). Never call ledger APIs directly from charge code.
patterns.example: ref, err := h.handler.OnCreditPurchaseInitiated(ctx, charge)

patterns.name: models.NewNillableGenericValidationError for Validate()
patterns.description: All Validate() methods return models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)) so nil is returned when errs is empty.
patterns.example: return models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...))

patterns.name: ValidationIssue sentinel errors
patterns.description: Domain-level errors are defined as package-level var using models.NewValidationIssue with ErrCode constant and commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute, not fmt.Errorf.
patterns.example: var ErrCreditPurchaseChargeNotActive = models.NewValidationIssue(ErrCodeCreditPurchaseChargeNotActive, ..., commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(http.StatusBadRequest))

key_file_guides.role: Defines Adapter (ChargeAdapter + CreditGrantAdapter + ExternalPaymentAdapter + InvoicedPaymentAdapter + TxCreator) and all input types with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every input type must have a Validate() method returning models.NewNillableGenericValidationError.

key_file_guides.role: Charge, ChargeBase, Intent, Settlement, Realizations, and state types. Intent.Normalized() truncates timestamps.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreditAmount must be positive; settlement currency must match credit currency; EffectiveAt is not yet supported and always errors.

key_file_guides.role: Settlement tagged-union with JSON serde. InvoiceSettlement, ExternalSettlement, PromotionalSettlement.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never instantiate Settlement{} directly; always use NewSettlement[T].

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface — single abstraction for all ledger side-effects. Promotional path only uses OnPromotionalCreditPurchase; paid paths go through Initiated→Authorized→Settled.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Handler methods must not be called outside the service layer.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface = CreditPurchaseService + ExternalPaymentLifecycle + InvoicePaymentLifecycle.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChargeWithGatheringLine is returned from Create; caller must process GatheringLineToCreate.

key_file_guides.role: Status enum mapping meta.ChargeStatus values for credit-purchase.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ToMetaChargeStatus() must be used to convert back to meta.ChargeStatus.

key_file_guides.role: ErrCreditPurchaseChargeNotActive sentinel with HTTP 400.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use this sentinel (errors.Is) rather than creating new errors for this condition.

key_file_guides.role: FundedCreditActivity, cursor-based pagination input/result for ListFundedCreditActivities.
key_file_guides.watch_for: After and Before cannot both be set; Limit must be >= 1.

anti_patterns: Calling ledger APIs directly instead of routing through creditpurchase.Handler — bypasses credits.enabled=false guard.
Constructing Settlement{} with direct field assignment instead of NewSettlement[T] — leaves discriminator field t empty.
Skipping intent.Normalized() before Validate() — allows denormalized timestamps and un-rounded amounts to reach the adapter.
Returning raw fmt.Errorf from Validate() instead of models.NewNillableGenericValidationError — breaks error-type detection upstream.
Setting EffectiveAt on Intent — it is explicitly unsupported and will return a validation error.

decisions.decision: Settlement is a tagged union (private fields + discriminator) rather than an interface
decisions.rationale: Enables exhaustive JSON serde and GetCostBasis() on all variants without type assertions at call sites.

decisions.decision: Handler interface decouples ledger writes from charge service
decisions.rationale: Allows noop handler when credits.enabled=false without branching inside service code.
Gathering line creation must be deferred to the caller (charges.Service) to fit the billing line lifecycle model without circular dependency.

code_examples.scenario: Create a credit purchase with invoice settlement

openmeter/billing/charges/lineage.purpose: Domain package for credit realization lineage tracking: defines Service and Adapter interfaces for creating initial lineages, persisting correction segments, and backfilling advance segments. Lineage records link credit realizations to their backing transaction groups via append-only Segment rows; mutations use close-then-split semantics rather than in-place updates.

patterns.name: transaction.RunWithNoValue for multi-step mutations
patterns.description: Service methods that call multiple adapter methods must wrap them in transaction.RunWithNoValue so all steps share the same ctx-bound transaction.
patterns.example: return transaction.RunWithNoValue(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) error { return s.adapter.CreateLineages(ctx, ...) })

patterns.name: Input.Validate() before business logic
patterns.description: Every service method calls input.Validate() as the first step before any adapter or lock operation.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Lock-then-mutate segment pattern
patterns.description: Adapter lock methods (LockCorrectionLineages, LockAdvanceLineagesForBackfill) acquire FOR UPDATE row locks inside an active transaction; must be called before reading segments that will be mutated.
patterns.example: lineages, err := s.adapter.LockCorrectionLineages(ctx, input.Namespace, realizationIDs)

patterns.name: Close-and-split for partial segment mutation
patterns.description: When only part of a segment's amount is consumed, close the existing segment (CloseSegment) and create two new segments rather than updating the amount in place.
patterns.example:
s.adapter.CloseSegment(ctx, seg.ID, clock.Now().UTC().Truncate(time.Microsecond))
s.adapter.CreateSegment(ctx, CreateSegmentInput{LineageID: seg.LineageID, Amount: consumed, ...})
s.adapter.CreateSegment(ctx, CreateSegmentInput{LineageID: seg.LineageID, Amount: remaining, ...})

patterns.name: clock.Now() with Truncate(time.Microsecond)
patterns.description: All timestamps used for segment closed_at must be truncated to microsecond precision to match Postgres storage precision and avoid equality-check failures.
patterns.example: closedAt := clock.Now().UTC().Truncate(time.Microsecond)

key_file_guides.role: Defines Lineage, Segment, ActiveSegmentsByRealizationID, and all input/output types. SortCorrectionPersistSegments and MinDecimal are pure helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: BackingTransactionGroupID is required for advance_backfilled segments; Segment.Amount must be positive.

key_file_guides.role: Service and Adapter interfaces; all input types with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: PersistCorrectionLineageSegmentsInput.Validate() only checks corrections; BackfillAdvanceLineageSegmentsInput requires positive Amount and non-empty BackingTransactionGroupID.

anti_patterns: Bypassing transaction.RunWithNoValue for multi-step adapter calls — creates partial writes when any step fails mid-sequence.
Calling adapter methods outside the transaction.RunWithNoValue closure — the closure's ctx carries the active tx driver; calls outside use a different connection.
Using clock.Now() without Truncate(time.Microsecond) for segment timestamps — Postgres microsecond precision mismatch causes equality check failures.
Adding Ent query logic directly in service.go — all DB access must go through lineage.Adapter methods.
Removing the GetDriverFromContext guard from lock methods in the adapter — makes FOR UPDATE locks unreliable outside explicit transactions.

decisions.decision: Close-and-split rather than update-in-place for segment mutation
decisions.rationale: Append-only segment history enables auditing and prevents lost-update races under concurrent backfill.

decisions.decision: Lock operations require an active transaction (GetDriverFromContext guard)
decisions.rationale: FOR UPDATE locks are only meaningful inside a transaction; failing fast prevents silent non-locking reads.

decisions.decision: Package-level LoadActiveSegmentsByRealizationID alongside the adapter method
decisions.rationale: Allows cross-package callers (creditpurchase service) to load segments without taking a full lineage.Service dependency.

openmeter/billing/charges/meta.purpose: Shared domain primitives for all charge types: ChargeID, ChargeType, ChargeStatus, ManagedResource, Intent, Expands, and the Patch interface (PatchDelete/Extend/Shrink) backed by stateless triggers. Also provides timestamp normalization (NormalizeTimestamp/NormalizeClosedPeriod) and the service.go type alias (Service = Adapter) — no separate service layer exists here.

patterns.name: Patch interface + stateless.Trigger
patterns.description: Every patch type implements Patch: Validate() + Trigger() stateless.Trigger + TriggerParams() any. Trigger constants live in triggers.go. New patch types must follow this pattern.

patterns.name: NormalizeTimestamp on all time.Time fields
patterns.description: All timestamps stored in Intent and patches must pass through NormalizeTimestamp (UTC + Truncate(streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration)) before persistence. Intent.Normalized() calls this for all period fields.
patterns.example: i.ServicePeriod = NormalizeClosedPeriod(i.ServicePeriod)

patterns.name: ChargeID = models.NamespacedID alias
patterns.description: ChargeID is a type alias of models.NamespacedID; validate with ChargeID.Validate() which delegates to NamespacedID.Validate().
patterns.example: func (i ChargeID) Validate() error { return models.NamespacedID(i).Validate() }

patterns.name: Service = Adapter type alias
patterns.description: meta.Service is a type alias for meta.Adapter — no separate service implementation exists. If business logic is needed here in the future, add transaction.Run and a proper service layer.
patterns.example: type Service = Adapter

patterns.name: ValidateWith for period bounds checking
patterns.description: PatchExtend and PatchShrink provide ValidateWith(intent Intent) to check new periods against existing intent periods. Always call this in addition to Validate().
patterns.example: if err := patch.ValidateWith(charge.Intent); err != nil { return err }

key_file_guides.role: ChargeID, ChargeType, ChargeStatus, Expand, Expands, ChargeAccessor, Charge, Charges enums and types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ExpandNone is nil (not empty slice); ChargeAccessor requires GetChargeID() and ErrorAttributes().

key_file_guides.role: Shared Intent struct embedded in all charge intents. Validates ManagedBy, CustomerID, Currency, all three periods, TaxConfig, Subscription, UniqueReferenceID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UniqueReferenceID must be nil or non-empty string; never empty string.

key_file_guides.role: Patch interface definition.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TriggerParams() any is required for stateless parameter passing.

key_file_guides.role: PatchDelete + PatchDeletePolicy (CreditRefundPolicy, InvoiceRefundPolicy). RefundAsCreditsDeletePolicy is the recommended default.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always set both refund policies; partial policy leaves the other at zero value which fails Validate().

key_file_guides.role: NormalizeTimestamp, NormalizeOptionalTimestamp, NormalizeClosedPeriod, Intent.Normalized().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Truncates to streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration, not time.Microsecond — distinct from the lineage package.

key_file_guides.role: All stateless.Trigger constants shared across charge state machines.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New triggers must be added here; never define trigger strings inline in state machine configuration files.

key_file_guides.role: Type alias Service = Adapter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No service layer exists — if complex orchestration is added, this alias must be removed and replaced with a proper service.

key_file_guides.role: ManagedResource embeds NamespacedModel + ManagedModel + ID. GetChargeID() derives ChargeID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ID must be non-empty; validate via ManagedResource.Validate().

anti_patterns: Defining charge-type-specific copies of Intent period fields instead of embedding meta.Intent.
Calling NormalizeTimestamp with different truncation precision than streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration.
Adding complex business logic to meta package — it is a shared primitives package; logic belongs in type-specific service packages.
Using string literals for trigger constants instead of the vars in triggers.go.
Creating a PatchDelete with only one of the two refund policies set — the other defaults to empty string and fails Validate().

decisions.decision: Service = Adapter type alias with no separate service layer
decisions.rationale: meta is a persistence-thin registry; until orchestration is needed here, an alias avoids premature abstraction.

decisions.decision: Patch interface uses stateless.Trigger rather than a string
decisions.rationale: Compile-time type safety with the stateless library; callers pass trigger + params to statemachine.Machine.FireAndActivate.
ClickHouse window aggregation requires consistent truncation granularity across all charge-related timestamps.

openmeter/billing/charges/models.purpose: Structural grouping for shared data-model sub-packages used by all charge types (flatfee, usagebased, creditpurchase). Sub-packages provide: chargemeta (Ent mixin + generic Create/Update/MapFromDB), creditrealization (allocation/correction ledger records), invoicedusage (accrued usage snapshots), ledgertransaction (GroupReference/TimedGroupReference value types), and payment (External/Invoiced payment models with Ent mixin). No direct source files live here.

patterns.name: chargemeta.Mixin embedding
patterns.description: All charge Ent schemas must embed chargemeta.Mixin via ent.Mixin() to get standard charge fields (customer_id, periods, status, currency, managed_by, subscription refs, advance_after). Never re-define these fields per charge type.
patterns.example: func (CreditPurchaseCharge) Mixin() []ent.Mixin { return []ent.Mixin{chargemeta.Mixin{}, ...} }

patterns.name: creditrealization.Mixin + SelfReferenceType
patterns.description: Charge entities that hold credit realizations must use creditrealization.Mixin with SelfReferenceType set to the entity's own type for self-referencing correction edges.
patterns.example: creditrealization.Mixin{SelfReferenceType: entschema.CreditPurchaseRealization{}}

patterns.name: totals.Mixin composition in invoicedusage
patterns.description: AccruedUsage totals fields must be added via totals.Mixin, not inline field definitions, and read/written via totals.Set/totals.FromDB.
patterns.example:
type AccruedUsageMixin struct { ent.Schema }
func (AccruedUsageMixin) Mixin() []ent.Mixin { return []ent.Mixin{totals.Mixin{}} }

patterns.name: payment.Base → External/Invoiced hierarchy
patterns.description: Payment entities share a common Base (status, currency, amount, authorized/settled refs) via payment.Mixin. Invoiced adds line_id/invoice_id (Immutable). Never embed Base fields directly in a new payment variant.
patterns.example: type ExternalPayment struct { payment.Base; ... }

patterns.name: ValidationIssue sentinels in payment/errors.go
patterns.description: All payment domain errors are defined as package-level var ValidationIssue with HTTP status codes, not fmt.Errorf. Use errors.Is to match them.
patterns.example: var ErrAlreadyAuthorized = models.NewValidationIssue(..., commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(http.StatusConflict))

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin providing all standard charge columns + generic Create/Update/MapFromDB.
key_file_guides.watch_for: intent.Normalized() + meta.NormalizeOptionalTimestamp are called at the top of Create/Update; never skip these.

key_file_guides.role: Realization, Realizations, InitialLineageSpec types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SortHint must be set to non-zero unique values per batch for deterministic correction ordering.

key_file_guides.role: Realizations.Correct() correction planning; amounts must be rounded via currencyx.Calculator before calling.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never manually set CorrectsRealizationID; use Realizations.Correct() which handles ID linking and remaining-amount calculation.

key_file_guides.role: GroupReference (TransactionGroupID) and TimedGroupReference. Nil-safe GetIDOrNull().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validate() before accessing TransactionGroupID; use GetIDOrNull() for SetNillable Ent fields.

key_file_guides.role: Status enum, Base type, CreateInput/UpdateInput interfaces.
key_file_guides.watch_for: StatusSettled requires AuthorizedAt to be non-nil; enforced in Base.Validate().

key_file_guides.role: Invoiced type with Immutable LineID/InvoiceID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: LineID and InvoiceID must not be mutated after creation; use UpdateInvoicedPayment only for status/transaction group fields.

anti_patterns: Defining per-charge-type copies of chargemeta fields instead of embedding chargemeta.Mixin.
Setting SortHint=0 on all realizations in a batch — correction ordering becomes non-deterministic.
Accessing ledgertransaction.TransactionGroupID without calling Validate() first.
Mutating Invoiced.LineID or Invoiced.InvoiceID after creation — these are Immutable Ent fields.
Creating a correction realization without going through Realizations.Correct() — bypasses CorrectsRealizationID assignment and amount validation.

decisions.decision: chargemeta.Mixin + generic Create/Update/MapFromDB instead of per-type boilerplate
decisions.rationale: Three charge types share identical persistence fields; type parameters eliminate duplication while keeping compile-time safety.

decisions.decision: Two-type realization model (allocation + correction) instead of mutable balance rows
decisions.rationale: Append-only model provides a full audit trail for credit reconciliation and avoids lost-update races.

decisions.decision: Separate Invoiced and External payment types sharing a payment.Base
decisions.rationale: Invoiced payments have an immutable invoice/line link not applicable to external payments; separate types prevent accidental mutation.

openmeter/billing/charges/statemachine.purpose: Generic charge state machine backed by the stateless library. Machine[CHARGE, BASE, STATUS] wraps a stateless.StateMachine with external storage bound to in-memory CHARGE, persisting via injected Persistence.UpdateBase after each successful transition. FireAndActivate fires a trigger and persists; AdvanceUntilStateStable walks TriggerNext transitions until no more are available. Used by all charge types (flatfee, usagebased, creditpurchase).

patterns.name: ChargeLike constraint for type parameter
patterns.description: CHARGE must implement ChargeLike[CHARGE, BASE, STATUS]: GetChargeID, GetStatus, WithStatus (pure copy), GetBase, WithBase (pure copy). STATUS must implement Status (~string + Validate()).
patterns.example:
func (c MyCharge) WithStatus(s MyStatus) MyCharge { c.Status = s; return c }
func (c MyCharge) WithBase(b MyBase) MyCharge { c.Base = b; return c }

patterns.name: FireAndActivate before AdvanceUntilStateStable
patterns.description: Use FireAndActivate for externally triggered transitions (e.g. payment authorized). Use AdvanceUntilStateStable to auto-advance through all TriggerNext transitions after an external event.
patterns.example:
if err := machine.FireAndActivate(ctx, meta.TriggerCollectionCompleted); err != nil { return err }
charge, err := machine.AdvanceUntilStateStable(ctx)

patterns.name: Persistence.UpdateBase stores BASE, not full CHARGE
patterns.description: UpdateBase persists only the BASE fields (status-bearing struct); Refetch loads the full CHARGE when edges must be reloaded after a transition.

patterns.name: AdvanceUntilStateStable returns nil when already stable
patterns.description: When the machine has no TriggerNext transition from the current state, AdvanceUntilStateStable returns (nil, nil). Callers must handle the nil charge pointer.

patterns.name: STATUS Validate() called on every state setter
patterns.description: The external state setter validates the incoming status via STATUS.Validate(). New status types must have exhaustive Validate() implementations — invalid statuses returned from transitions will be caught here.

key_file_guides.role: Machine[CHARGE,BASE,STATUS], Config, Persistence, ChargeLike constraint, StateMachine interface, New() constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FireAndActivate calls Activate after Fire to trigger OnActive callbacks before persisting; activation errors skip persistence.

key_file_guides.role: Reference implementations of ChargeLike (fakeCharge/fakeBase/fakeStatus) and test patterns for FireAndActivate, AdvanceUntilStateStable, and RefetchCharge.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use t.Context() not context.Background(); use these as templates for new charge type state machine tests.

anti_patterns: Implementing WithStatus or WithBase as pointer receivers that mutate in place — they must return a new value copy because Machine.Charge is updated by assignment.
Calling m.stateMachine.FireCtx directly instead of FireAndActivate — skips the CanFire guard, Activate call, and persistence step.
Omitting OnActive callbacks when the state requires side effects on entry — activation runs between Fire and Persist.
Returning an invalid STATUS value from a transition target — the external state setter's Validate() will catch it but only at runtime.
Ignoring the nil return from AdvanceUntilStateStable — nil means no transitions were fired, not an error.

decisions.decision: Generic Machine[CHARGE, BASE, STATUS] instead of per-type state machines
decisions.rationale: Three charge types share identical transition mechanics (fire, activate, persist-base, refetch); generics eliminate duplication while keeping type safety.
Charge status is stored in the domain struct, not inside the stateless library; this keeps the machine stateless and allows refetch without re-creating the machine.
Base fields (status, schedule fields) are what must be durable after a transition; realizations and edges are loaded on demand via Refetch.

code_examples.scenario: Configure and drive a charge state machine
